subject: Public Health
title: The Global Water Crisis
description: A 60-second lesson on The Global Water Crisis.

hook.text: 2.2 billion people lack safely managed drinking water—that's more than one in four humans alive today.

buildup.text: Many walk hours daily to collect water from rivers, ponds, or shared wells—often contaminated.

discovery.text: Women and girls bear the burden—they perform 72% of all water collection worldwide.

twist.text: Climate change is making it worse—droughts, floods, and glacier loss threaten existing water sources.

climax.text: By 2050, half the world's population could face water scarcity during at least one month per year.

punchline.text: Water covers 71% of Earth. Only 1% is drinkable and accessible.

quiz.question: Who performs 72% of all water collection globally?
